claim_boundary: The route proves only canonical generation and review for the declared finite universe; downstream execution and broad confidence remain separate.
do_not_use_when: The boundary is undeclared or unbounded, the task is test execution, or another route's semantic ownership would be duplicated.

downstream_targets.condition: Generated cases must become model obligations and owner-code test targets.
Parent confidence must consume child coverage receipts.
Generated coverage supports a broad final claim.

route_summary: Generate canonical finite-boundary cases, oracles, Cartesian combinations, shards, receipts, and typed consumers.

source_requirements.summary: Every case must derive from an owner-declared finite dimension, seed, axis/group, or universe item.
Required cases need actionable oracles and current shards/receipts/backfeed mappings for the claimed scope.
Generated matrix readiness cannot be reported as MTA, TestMesh, ModelMesh, or risk-ledger closure.

use_when: An owner has declared a finite boundary, family seed, payload, transition, mesh hazard, universe, or observed-problem backfeed.

workflow.summary: Identify the declaring owner, model scope, finite boundary, claim scope, and required consumers.
Inventory dimensions, seeds, axes/groups, coverage universe, exclusions, oracles, child receipts, and backfeed.
Generate stable mutation/combination cases, feedback fields, shards, receipts, and composite acceptances.
Run contract-exhaustion tests and reject missing universe items, incomplete oracles, stale receipts, or unmapped misses.
Project case/obligation/cell/shard/receipt/risk ids to typed consumers without claiming downstream pass.
